(reported figure: 282000%) Honestly not surprised given what's been happening lately. Share this before it gets taken down. This is huge.

corpus.title: News: Upper Dawnton River Basin Authority — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: The Upper Dawnton River Basin restoration project removed 94,000 metric tons of legacy phosphorus se...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Upper Dawnton River Basin Authority makes a significant move amid changes in the environmental sector

The Upper Dawnton River Basin restoration project removed 94,000 metric tons of legacy phosphorus sediment between 2020 and 2023, reducing harmful algal bloom frequency by 67%. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in environmental strategy. A spokesperson for Upper Dawnton River Basin Authority declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: Upper Dawnton River Basin Restoration Programme — Phase II Completion Report
corpus.source: Upper Dawnton River Basin Authority / EPA Region 5
corpus.snippet: The Upper Dawnton River Basin restoration project removed 94,000 metric tons of legacy phosphorus sediment between 2020 ...
corpus.content:
UPPER DAWNTON RIVER BASIN AUTHORITY
Phase II Restoration Programme — Completion and Outcome Report
Co-published with EPA Region 5 | February 12, 2024

PROGRAMME SUMMARY
The Phase II Sediment Remediation Programme (2020-2023) targeted legacy phosphorus-laden sediment deposited during historical agricultural and industrial discharge across the Upper Dawnton River Basin (1,840 km² catchment area).

REMEDIATION OUTCOMES
Total phosphorus-laden sediment removed: 94,000 metric tons
Removal method: Hydraulic dredging (63%) and mechanical capping (37%)
River reaches treated: 148 km of main stem and major tributaries
Cost: $127M (federal: $89M, state: $28M, local: $10M)

ECOLOGICAL RESPONSE (2020-2023 monitoring data)
Harmful algal bloom (HAB) events per season:
  Baseline (2017-2019 average): 18 events/season
  Post-remediation (2023): 6 events/season
  Reduction: -12 events (-66.7%, rounded to 67%)

Total phosphorus concentration (main stem, August mean):
  Baseline: 0.18 mg/L
  2023: 0.09 mg/L (-50%)

Dissolved oxygen compliance (>5 mg/L threshold): 91% of monitoring days in 2023 vs. 58% baseline. Macroinvertebrate index scores improved from 'Poor' to 'Fair' in 11 of 14 monitored reaches.
